results.possibleIdentity: George Horiuchi was an engineer and acoustics consultant active in New York City during the 1950s and 1960s. He specialized in electroacoustics, acoustics, and audio-visual displays. In the late 1950s, he served as an educational advisor for the Audio Engineering Society (AES) on publications such as "Principles and Practices of Tape and Disc Recording" (1958) and was associated with the Journal of the Audio Engineering Society, with an address listed at Columbia University. In January 1969, he participated as a performer in "Chorus for Six Hearts," a collaboration by artist Jean Dupuy featured in the "The Machine and Nature" audio-visual concert at the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA). This event was held in conjunction with the exhibition "The Machine as Seen at the End of the Mechanical Age" and was sponsored by Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.). During this period, he also served as a consultant in acoustics for the Soundcoat Co. in New York.

webSearchQueries: George Horiuchi "Experiments in Art and Technology"
